title: [Feature]: Evaluate an Amazon DocumentDB sink
problem: Oracle GoldenGate Big Data targets include AWS DocumentDB. nats-sinks has a MongoDB sink backlog item, but it does not yet capture Amazon DocumentDB compatibility, TLS, and API-difference risks.
proposal: Evaluate whether Amazon DocumentDB should be a compatibility profile under a future MongoDB sink or a dedicated connector. The design should cover TLS certificate handling, connection strings, write concerns, retryable writes support, deterministic document IDs, and compatibility limitations versus MongoDB.
users: AWS users storing event envelopes in Amazon DocumentDB while preserving nats-sinks idempotency and metadata contracts.
delivery_semantics: Success may be returned only after the configured write concern reports durable acceptance. Duplicate redelivery must map to deterministic document IDs or a documented duplicate policy.
security: Connection strings, certificates, users, payloads, document IDs, and collection names must be redacted. TLS verification must be enabled by default.
acceptance: Research compares Amazon DocumentDB behavior with MongoDB sink requirements.
The design decides profile versus dedicated sink.
Configuration validates database, collection, TLS, and duplicate settings.
Unit tests use fake clients and avoid network calls.
tests: Add mocked client tests for config validation, duplicate document handling, write concern choices, and error translation.
documentation: Document as a MongoDB compatibility profile or a dedicated sink page depending on the decision.
closeout: Close after release of the decision or implementation.
